binding_constraint.reason: Schema union implementation is the hardest-to-close precondition and blocks all three P0 fixes in the non-software path; RT-5 (headings), RT-10 (fresh-run acceptance), and RT-7 (additive-only) cannot proceed until the discriminated union is in place. It is also the single ONE_WAY risk in an otherwise reversible plan — the shape choice, once shipped, is expensive to reverse because user-authored non-software manifolds will be written against whichever keys land first.

generation.execution_depth: B (plan + tests + new files; EDITs pending)

iterations.result: Discovered 14 constraints across 4 categories (security empty by design — no new auth/crypto surface). Draft derived from tests/golden/prompt-eval-report.md P0/P1/P2 findings; user accepted draft as-is. Pre-mortem stress-test added O4 (fresh-run validation gate). Web context lookup skipped — feature is internal meta-work on own prompts.

Surfaced 3 tensions: TN1 (schema contract, resolved Option A — schema union by domain), TN2 (m3 variance vs body size, resolved via split+flat elicitation), TN3 (m5 artifact vs harness strictness, resolved via carve-out). All resolutions passed propagation check with no VIOLATED effects. TN1 Option A locked in via user decision; propagation tightens B2 (mitigated) and loosens U1. Web context lookup skipped — same session as m1, context preserved.

Derived 10 required truths (RT-1 decomposed into 3 children). Binding constraint is RT-1 (schema union impl); dependency chain RT-1 → RT-5 → RT-10 → RT-7. Recommended Option A (Schema-first sequential). All 3 tensions CONFIRMED by Option A — no reopening. Solution Option A tagged TWO_WAY per step; the schema shape choice itself is ONE_WAY once published and is gated on explicit user acknowledgment via the binding-constraint callout. Web context lookup skipped — meta-work on own prompts.

Generated 13 NEW files (9 tests, 1 reference, 2 planning docs, 1 runbook) and staged 7 PENDING EDITs with precise diffs in docs/prompt-eval-fixes-v2/DECISIONS.md. Execution depth B selected — tests land first so each EDIT can be validated against its test. Binding-constraint artifacts (schema-union.test.ts and fresh-run-non-software.test.ts) generated first per RT-1 priority. ONE_WAY schema EDIT (step 1) remains staged for explicit user acknowledgment and review before application. Evidence populated on all 10 RTs; every artifact declares artifact_class. Coverage 93% (13/14 constraints addressed; S category empty by design).

PARTIAL — schema validates, 13/13 generated artifacts present, 6/7 pending-EDIT paths present (install/hooks/phase-commons missing; logged as G8 advisory). Evidence summary: 4 items VERIFIED (2 file_exists + 2 content_match), 9 items PENDING (test_passes not executed). Per the TESTED-vs-IMPLEMENTED rule, PENDING test_passes items count as IMPLEMENTED only. 1 constraint SATISFIED (O1, RT-6). 13 constraints PARTIAL. Binding constraint RT-1 NOT_SATISFIED until schema EDIT 1 (ONE_WAY) lands. Phase stays GENERATED; .verify.json emitted unconditionally per TN3 resolution. Level breakdown: DOCUMENTED=1, IMPLEMENTED=12, TESTED=0, VERIFIED=1.

PASS — all 7 EDITs applied (EDITs 1-6 shipped; EDIT 7 deferred as optional). 19/19 artifacts present, 17/17 evidence items VERIFIED (file_exists + content_match + test_passes executed via --run-tests). All 10 required truths (including children RT-1.1, RT-1.2, RT-1.3) flipped from NOT_SATISFIED/PARTIAL/SPECIFICATION_READY to SATISFIED. Binding constraint RT-1 (schema discriminated union) resolved — ONE_WAY EDIT acknowledged and landed. Phase advanced to VERIFIED, convergence to CONVERGED. All 48 prompt-template + schema tests green. Obsolete artifact install/hooks/phase-commons removed from plan since template-audit approach satisfied RT-9 directly.

reversibility_log.description: Ship zod discriminated union on `domain` with software + non-software shapes
reversibility_log.one_way_consequence: Once non-software manifolds are authored against the new keys, reversing the key choice would require user data migration — publishing is the commit point.

Improve manifold validate error messages to include field path and required pattern

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: Meta feature — prompt template → LLM emission → schema validator flow is implicitly the data path; covered by T1, T2, T3, O1
No auth/crypto constraints in this feature
No unauthenticated endpoints or runtime paths
No external HTTP dependencies beyond LLM calls, which the harness already manages with rate-cap awareness

gap_checklist_compliance.notes: T1 + T2 + O1 codify the input validation boundary: the schema is the validator, manifold validate is the gate

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: No shared runtime state — prompt templates are static markdown artifacts

draft_required_truths.draft_statement: Schema has a canonical, unambiguous representation for non-software domain categories (either extended keys or documented semantics)
Every evidence emission snippet in m3-anchor.md and m4-generate.md templates includes the id field
Every iteration emission snippet in m1-m5 skills includes result: string, OR phase-commons hook injects it when missing
m5-verify.md emits .verify.json on every completion code path, independent of phase decision (SATISFIED, PARTIAL, or blocked)
m0-init --domain=non-software produces markdown category headings matching CLAUDE.md universal vocabulary (Obligations/Desires/Resources/Risks/Dependencies)
manifold validate remains the single schema enforcement gate; no parallel or competing validators introduced
Schema changes are additive-only — no new required fields on existing manifold shapes that would break the 19-manifold software corpus
m3-anchor variance is reduced either by constraining the --depth default or by splitting recursive-decomposition behind references/ so it loads only when depth > 1
